title: Denali Wolf Monitoring Annual Report 2024
description: The most recent annual summary of wolf population monitoring in Denali National Park and Preserve, including pack counts, territory data, and collaring efforts.

title: Denali Wolf Monitoring Annual Report 2023
description: Annual summary of wolf population monitoring in Denali National Park and Preserve.

description: Annual summary of wolf population monitoring in Denali National Park and Preserve.

description: Annual summary of wolf population monitoring in Denali National Park and Preserve.

description: Annual summary of wolf population monitoring in Denali National Park and Preserve.

title: Denali Wolf Sighting Index, 1997 - 2019
description: A long-term index of wolf sighting rates along the Denali Park Road, documenting trends in wolf viewing opportunity from 1997 through 2019.

title: Denali Wolf Pack Narratives
description: Individual pack histories and narratives compiled from Denali Wolf Project monitoring records, covering territory, composition, and notable events for named packs.

title: Implications of Harvest on the Boundaries of Protected Areas for Large Carnivore Viewing Opportunities
organization: National Park Service / University of Washington
description: Peer-reviewed study examining how wolf harvest outside Denali and Yellowstone boundaries affects the frequency of wolf sightings within the parks. Finds that a protective buffer nearly doubled sighting rates in Denali.

title: Suspected True Hermaphroditism in a Free-ranging Gray Wolf (Canis lupus) in Interior Alaska, USA
description: Peer-reviewed study of  the behavior and physical and anatomic appearance of a suspected true hermaphroditic free-ranging wild gray wolf in Denali National Park and Preserve. The study describes the wolf's intersexual characteristics, behavior, and social interactions within its pack, contributing to the understanding of hermaphroditism in wild canids.

title: The Wolves of Mount McKinley
organization: U.S. Department of the Interior / National Park Service
description: The foundational scientific study of wolves in what is now Denali National Park, based on field observations from 1939 - 1941. Murie's meticulous work helped establish the ecological importance of wolves and influenced decades of wildlife management policy.

title: The Wolves of Denali
description: A scientific study of Denali's wolf population, based on over nine years of intensive research from 1986 - 1995. Covers all aspects of wolf ecology including pack organization, prey relationships with caribou, Dall sheep, and moose, and population dynamics.

title: Among Wolves: Gordon Haber's Insights into Alaska's Most Misunderstood Animal
description: Published posthumously, this book presents Haber's four decades of field research and advocacy in Denali. Haber documented the social complexity of wolf packs and was a prominent critic of wolf hunting and trapping near the park.

title: The Wanderer: An Alaska Wolf's Final Journey
description: The first book to chart a wolf's movements almost to the day for an extended period, following GPS-collared Wolf 258, dubbed 'the Wanderer', across more than 2,700 miles of Alaskan wilderness in under six months. Drawn from two decades of wolf research at Yukon - Charley Rivers National Preserve, Walker weaves natural history, Indigenous history, and the politics of wolf management into one wolf's remarkable final journey.

title: Bill Watkins Conservation - YouTube Channel
description: YouTube channel and conservation media by Bill Watkins, a 36-year Denali Tundra Wilderness Tour guide, wildlife photographer, and wolf advocate. Watkins has documented Denali wolves since 1990 and has been a prominent voice in the effort to restore harvest protections in the Stampede Corridor.

title: Denali Wolves - Facebook Group
description: Public Facebook group moderated by Bill Watkins with regular posts on wolf sightings, conservation news, and the ongoing situation in the Stampede Corridor. An active community gathering point for Denali wolf advocates and observers.

title: A Good Wolf
description: A feature-length documentary exploring the 40-year conflict over wolf management in the Stampede Corridor adjacent to Denali National Park. Told through three perspectives, wildlife advocates, local hunters and trappers, and NPS biologists, the film follows Wolf 1202, an aging matriarch, as humans battle over the fate of her territory. Directed by Ramey Newell.

title: NPS IRMA DataStore - Denali & Yukon - Charley Wolf Resources
description: The NPS Integrated Resource Management Applications DataStore is the authoritative repository for Denali and Yukon-Charley wolf monitoring reports, datasets, and research publications. Searching by park unit surfaces a broad range of wolf-related projects and reports. A deep resource for those with time to explore.

title: Denali Wolf Project - NPS Official Page
description: The official NPS page for the Denali Wolf Project, providing an overview of monitoring methods, research history, and current status.
